meaning: n.餐具柜,小卖部,殴打,打击v.打击,搏斗
examples: Dinner will be a cold buffet, not a sit-down meal.
At lunchtime, there's a choice between the buffet or the set menu.
It is a buffet, right? I love wedding buffets.
It is hard to make a profit feeding obese people at an "all-you-can-eat" buffet.
Other dollars support buffet coupons and free shuttle buses to the Rio, Harrah's fanciest casino.
